title: Debug a kernel oops or panic
description: Turn a crash into a fix by decoding the stack trace, identifying the faulting access, and reproducing under sanitizers in a VM.
body: Steps: 1. Capture the oops/panic log and decode the call trace and faulting address (addr2line / decode_stacktrace). 2. Classify it: NULL/bad-pointer deref, use-after-free, deadlock, or BUG assertion. 3. Reproduce in a VM with KASAN/lockdep/kmemleak enabled to pinpoint the access. 4. Trace back to the missing check, race, or unbalanced free. 5. Fix the root cause and re-run the sanitized stress test. Rollback point: keep the change in a VM you can crash, not on hardware you depend on. Validation: the sanitizer pinpoints a clean root cause and the crash no longer reproduces under stress; silencing a BUG/WARN without understanding the access only hides the fault.
